project.name: InfoScreen
project.purpose: Local kiosk dashboard for a Surface or Ubuntu display.

document_contract.metadata.json: Project requirements, constraints, implementation plan, and acceptance criteria.
document_contract.docs/api-spec.md: HTTP endpoint interactions and request response contracts.
document_contract.docs/design.md: Concrete system design contracts and verification gates.
document_contract.docs/questions.md: Decision rationale, judgment criteria, alternatives, and change conditions.

constraints: Runtime paths require verification on the running Surface host before change.
Mac schedule sync is independent from Surface frontend and crawler branches.
serve_infoscreen.py serves files and APIs only.
Checked-in frontend CSS and JS live under surface/web/assets/css and surface/web/assets/js.
HTTP file logging remains enabled unless replaced by a documented logging migration.
Runtime files must be backed up before source file cleanup on the deployed Surface host.
Docs must not contain guessed paths.

target_repository_layout.mac/: macOS Apple Calendar export and schedule sync.
target_repository_layout.scripts/: Developer, CI, status, and setup scripts.
target_repository_layout.surface/: Surface app implementation and runtime Python entrypoints.

acceptance_criteria: Schedule endpoint returns the verified Surface schedule file.
Frontend layout uses one state owner per panel and canonical assets paths.
HTTP file logs are written under surface/.env/logs/.
docs/ contains only api-spec.md, design.md, and questions.md.
Runtime and generated files are ignored and not committed.
